heading: Discharge of indebtedness
label: 1017 Discharge of indebtedness

children.text: an amount is excluded from gross income under subsection (a) of section 108 (relating to discharge of indebtedness), and
under subsection (b)(2)(E), (b)(5), or (c)(1) of section 108, any portion of such amount is to be applied to reduce basis,

children.heading: Amount and properties determined under regulations
children.label: b Amount and properties determined under regulations

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The amount of reduction to be applied under subsection (a) (not in excess of the portion referred to in subsection (a)), and the particular properties the bases of which are to be reduced, shall be determined under regulations prescribed by the Secretary.

children.heading: Limitation in title 11 case or insolvency
children.label: 2 Limitation in title 11 case or insolvency
A
children.text: the aggregate of the bases of the property held by the taxpayer immediately after the discharge, over

children.label: B
children.text: the aggregate of the liabilities of the taxpayer immediately after the discharge.

children.heading: Certain reductions may only be made in the basis of depreciable property
children.label: 3 Certain reductions may only be made in the basis of depreciable property

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Any amount which under subsection (b)(5) or (c)(1) of section 108 is to be applied to reduce basis shall be applied only to reduce the basis of depreciable property held by the taxpayer.

children.heading: Depreciable property
children.label: B Depreciable property
children.text: For purposes of this section, the term “depreciable property” means any property of a character subject to the allowance for depreciation, but only if a basis reduction under subsection (a) will reduce the amount of depreciation or amortization which otherwise would be allowable for the period immediately following such reduction.

children.heading: Special rule for partnership interests
children.label: C Special rule for partnership interests
children.text: For purposes of this section, any interest of a partner in a partnership shall be treated as depreciable property to the extent of such partner’s proportionate interest in the depreciable property held by such partnership. The preceding sentence shall apply only if there is a corresponding reduction in the partnership’s basis in depreciable property with respect to such partner.

children.heading: Special rule in case of affiliated group
children.label: D Special rule in case of affiliated group
i
children.text: a corporation holds stock in another corporation (hereinafter in this subparagraph referred to as the “subsidiary”), and

children.label: ii
children.text: such corporations are members of the same affiliated group which file a consolidated return under section 1501 for the taxable year in which the discharge occurs,

children.heading: Election to treat certain inventory as depreciable property
children.label: E Election to treat certain inventory as depreciable property

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: At the election of the taxpayer, for purposes of this section, the term “depreciable property” includes any real property which is described in section 1221(a)(1).

children.heading: Election
children.label: ii Election
children.text: An election under clause (i) shall be made on the taxpayer’s return for the taxable year in which the discharge occurs or at such other time as may be permitted in regulations prescribed by the Secretary. Such an election, once made, may be revoked only with the consent of the Secretary.

children.heading: Special rules for qualified real property business indebtedness
children.label: F Special rules for qualified real property business indebtedness
i
children.text: depreciable property shall only include depreciable real property for purposes of subparagraphs (A) and (C),

children.label: ii
children.text: subparagraph (E) shall not apply, and

children.label: iii
children.text: in the case of property taken into account under section 108(c)(2)(B), the reduction with respect to such property shall be made as of the time immediately before disposition if earlier than the time under subsection (a).

children.heading: Special rules for qualified farm indebtedness
children.label: 4 Special rules for qualified farm indebtedness
i
children.text: shall be applied only to reduce the basis of qualified property held by the taxpayer, and

children.label: I
children.text: First the basis of qualified property which is depreciable property.

children.label: II
children.text: Second the basis of qualified property which is land used or held for use in the trade or business of farming.

children.label: III
children.text: Then the basis of other qualified property.

children.heading: Qualified property
children.label: B Qualified property
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the term “qualified property” has the meaning given to such term by section 108(g)(3)(C).

children.heading: Certain rules made applicable
children.label: C Certain rules made applicable
children.text: Rules similar to the rules of subparagraphs (C), (D), and (E) of paragraph (3) shall apply for purposes of this paragraph and section 108(g).

children.heading: Reduction not to be made in exempt property
children.label: 1 Reduction not to be made in exempt property
children.text: In the case of an amount excluded from gross income under section 108(a)(1)(A), no reduction in basis shall be made under this section in the basis of property which the debtor treats as exempt property under of the United States Code.section 522 of title 11

children.heading: Reductions in basis not treated as dispositions
children.label: 2 Reductions in basis not treated as dispositions
children.text: For purposes of this title, a reduction in basis under this section shall not be treated as a disposition.

children.label: A
children.text: any property the basis of which is reduced under this section and which is neither section 1245 property nor section 1250 property shall be treated as section 1245 property, and

children.label: B
children.text: any reduction under this section shall be treated as a deduction allowed for depreciation.

children.heading: Special rule for section 1250
children.label: 2 Special rule for section 1250
children.text: For purposes of section 1250(b), the determination of what would have been the depreciation adjustments under the straight line method shall be made as if there had been no reduction under this section.
